title: Odontites rigidifolius
taxonomy: Odontites rigidifolius is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae, commonly known as the broomrape family. This family is a group of parasitic plants that obtain their nutrients by connecting to the roots of other plants. Odontites rigidifolius is further classified within the genus Odontites, which is a genus of annual plants. The species Odontites rigidifolius is native to certain regions of Europe, where it grows in dry to moderately dry habitats. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics as a parasitic plant with adapted growth habits.
uses:
Odontites rigidifolius is a plant species with reported traditional medicinal uses. 
Its leaves are used in some cultures for treating fever and other illnesses. 
The plant's properties have been studied for potential antiseptic and antimicrobial effects.

intro: Odontites rigidifolius, also known as stiff-leaved odontites, is a perennial flowering plant in the balsam family (Boraginaceae) that is native to Europe and western Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Odontites rigidifolius include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Odontites rigidifolius is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues and other health conditions.
